tldr: The US Supreme Court struck down limits on political parties' campaign spending, a decision seen as a win for the Republican party.
The Supreme Court also upheld birthright citizenship, blocking a Trump administration executive order, while separately upholding bans on transgender women in female school and college sports.
Forecasts indicate a US heatwave could strain the power grid due to soaring AI-driven energy demand ahead of the Fourth of July weekend.

market.why: The S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite both saw increases as of their most recent close on 2026-06-30. Small-cap stocks experienced their best first half in 35 years, driven by the expanding artificial intelligence trade, contributing to overall market optimism. Additionally, the Supreme Court's decision to strike down limits on political campaign spending, perceived as a victory for the GOP, may have influenced investor sentiment.

yield_10y.why: The 10-year Treasury yield increased as of its most recent close on 2026-06-30. This rise may be attributed to comments from Cleveland Fed President Beth Hammack, who suggested that artificial intelligence could fuel inflation and potentially necessitate future interest rate hikes.

vix.why: The VIX decreased as of its most recent close on 2026-06-30. This decline reflects a reduction in perceived market volatility and an increase in investor confidence, coinciding with the positive performance of the S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite.

tech.summary: Amazon is investing $1 billion to deploy engineers directly to clients, aiming to boost artificial-intelligence adoption by following Palantir's AI playbook.
Anthropic has released Claude Sonnet 5, an updated version of its artificial intelligence model.
A study suggests that the US achieving 100% electric vehicle adoption by 2040 could save over 100,000 lives, primarily by reducing diesel emissions from heavy-duty trucks and buses.

world.summary: The US Supreme Court delivered a landmark ruling, striking down limits on political parties' campaign spending, a decision seen as a win for the GOP.
The United Nations warned that the latest Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo could cost Africa $3.6 billion and result in job losses.
A looming US heatwave is expected to test the power grid, with operators warning that electricity demand could reach near-record levels, partly due to soaring AI-driven energy consumption.
